text: ECLASS16 			PreferredName	Definition	IRDI	CodedName	Level Electric engineering, automation, process control engineering				this classification encompasses all aspects related to the design, development, implementation, and management of electronic systems and processes aimed at enhancing control and automation in various applications. It includes devices, technologies, and methodologies that facilitate the regulation and monitoring of processes, ensuring efficiency and reliability in operations. The scope covers components and systems used in electrical engineering, emphasizing automation technologies, process control solutions, and related services. This classification differentiates itself by its focus on the integration of electrical components with automated systems, providing solutions for a wide range of industries that require sophisticated control mechanisms and process optimization. The aim is to support advancements in technology that lead to improved productivity and operational effectiveness.	0173-1#01-AAB572#010	27000000	1 	Connector system			this category encompasses systems designed to establish electrical connections between various components or devices within electrical engineering applications. It includes a range of elements that facilitate the transmission of power and signals, ensuring reliable communication and functionality in automation and control processes. The classification includes various types of connectors and associated components that serve distinct purposes in connection management, ranging from simple plug connections to more complex integration solutions. This classification is crucial for understanding the diverse applications and technologies involved in connecting electrical systems, supporting effective design and implementation in various sectors of automation and process control engineering.	0173-1#01-ADN096#010	27440000	2 		Industry connector		this classification encompasses a range of components designed to facilitate connections within various industrial applications. These elements are typically utilized in systems where robust and reliable interconnectivity is essential, particularly in automation and control technologies. The offerings include configurations for both sensor and actuator interfaces, which are critical in monitoring and controlling processes. Additionally, the classification features connectors suited for photovoltaic systems, enhancing energy efficiency and sustainability. The range incorporates various mounting types and assembly options to meet specific installation requirements, ensuring compatibility and ease of use in diverse environments. Overall, this classification serves to delineate specialized connection solutions that cater to the unique needs of industrial applications.	0173-1#01-ADS297#010	27440100	3 			Sensor-actuator connector chassis (sensor technology acturator)	recessed circular connectors are assembled connectors for front wall or rear wall mounting with cable connected	0173-1#01-ACN874#019	27440103	4 			Valve connector	Valve connection technology comprises connectors that enable safe connection in hydraulic and pneumatic areas of industrial application with solenoid valves and pressure switches	0173-1#01-AFR414#011	27440105	4 			Sensor actuator adapter	Distributor for data, signals and power (Y, T, H, and h-distribution). These items are characterized by their ability to enable or enhance connectivity within various systems, ensuring compatibility and functionality across different applications. The focus is on specialized connectors that serve distinct purposes in communication, data transfer, and power distribution. This includes a variety of styles and configurations tailored for specific uses in both industrial and commercial environments, supporting a range of electrical standards and technologies. Emphasis is placed on the versatility and adaptability of these connections in complex systems.	0173-1#01-ADS299#010	27440300	3 			Chipcard connector	a chipcard connector is a specialized interface designed to establish a secure and reliable connection between chip cards and electronic devices. It facilitates the transfer of data and power, ensuring that the chip card can communicate effectively with the host system. This type of connector is characterized by its optimized design for low-profile applications, often incorporating features that enhance durability and performance in various environments. Chipcard connectors are commonly used in payment systems, identification cards, and other applications where secure access and data integrity are critical. The design may include mechanisms for ease of insertion and removal, as well as protection against physical wear and environmental factors.	0173-1#01-BAC440#020	27440301	4 			Communication-connector	a type of interface device designed to establish electrical connections between various communication systems and equipment. This category encompasses components that facilitate data transmission and signal routing, ensuring compatibility and effective communication across different platforms. These devices may vary in design, configuration, and application, serving functions such as linking cables, interfacing with circuit boards, or enabling connectivity in telecommunications. Their primary purpose is to ensure reliable and efficient interaction within communication networks, supporting both analog and digital signals across a range of environments.	0173-1#01-ADT246#015	27440303	4 			Round/flat plug	a round/flat plug is an electrical interface designed to connect two circuit points, facilitating the transfer of electrical power or signals. This type of connector typically features a circular or flat-shaped design that allows for secure and reliable connections. The configuration is engineered to ensure proper alignment and engagement, minimizing the risk of misconnection or signal loss. It is commonly utilized in various applications across multiple industries, serving as a critical component in ensuring efficient electrical connectivity. The design may include features such as locking mechanisms or protective casings to enhance durability and user safety.	0173-1#01-AFZ685#023	27440304	4 			FO connector	a type of electrical connection device specifically designed for optical fiber applications. This connector facilitates the joining of optical fibers, enabling the transmission of light signals with minimal loss and optimal performance. It is characterized by its precise alignment mechanism, which ensures effective coupling of fiber ends. The design may include features for quick and efficient assembly and disassembly, as well as durability to withstand environmental factors. This type of connector is commonly used in telecommunications, data networks, and various applications requiring high-speed data transfer through fiber optics.	0173-1#01-BAC426#020	27440305	4 			RF connector	a type of electrical component designed to establish a reliable and efficient connection between radio frequency transmission lines and devices. This connector facilitates the transfer of signals with minimal loss and interference, ensuring optimal performance in various applications involving wireless communication, broadcasting, and networking. Typically characterized by specific impedance, physical design, and coupling mechanisms, this component is essential in systems requiring high-frequency signal integrity. Its usage spans across different industries, including telecommunications, automotive, aerospace, and consumer electronics, where seamless connectivity is paramount. This classification uniquely identifies connectors that are tailored for RF applications while distinguishing them from other types of connectors based on their specific functionalities and performance criteria.	0173-1#01-BAC429#020	27440306	4 			I/O connector	an I/O connector is a device that facilitates the connection and communication between different electronic components or systems. It serves as an interface that allows for the transmission of data, power, or signals, enabling the interaction between devices. These connectors are designed to support various input and output functions, making them essential in a wide range of applications, from consumer electronics to industrial machinery. The design of I/O connectors can vary based on the specific requirements of the application, including factors such as size, shape, and the type of signals being transmitted. They are critical for ensuring reliable and efficient connectivity in both wired and wireless communication systems.	0173-1#01-BAC428#020	27440307	4 			Cable connector (medium voltage)	a medium voltage cable connector is designed to facilitate the connection of electrical cables in medium voltage systems, ensuring reliable transmission of electrical power. This component typically incorporates robust materials and engineering to withstand higher voltage levels and environmental stresses. It is essential for maintaining electrical integrity and safety in various applications, including industrial and utility sectors. The connector allows for efficient and secure linking of cable ends, providing features such as insulation, sealing, and ease of installation. Its design is optimized for performance and durability in demanding conditions, ensuring longevity and operational reliability in the electrical infrastructure.	0173-1#01-ACN466#019	27440308	4 			Coax-Connector	a coaxial connector is a type of electrical connector designed for use with coaxial cables, facilitating the transmission of radio frequency signals. It typically consists of a central conductor, an insulating layer, an outer conductor, and a protective sheath. These connectors ensure minimal signal loss and interference, making them ideal for applications such as telecommunications, broadcasting, and networking. Coaxial connectors are characterized by their specific design parameters, which allow for secure and reliable connections in various environments. They can come in different configurations and sizes, tailored to meet the requirements of specific applications, and are crucial for maintaining signal integrity in high-frequency systems.	0173-1#01-ADT248#015	27440310	4 			Modular connector (industrial connector)	a modular connector is a type of interconnection device designed for industrial applications, facilitating the reliable transmission of electrical signals or power between various equipment and systems. Characterized by its versatile design, this connector allows for easy assembly, disassembly, and customization, making it ideal for use in environments where equipment may require frequent reconfiguration or maintenance. Modular connectors are typically constructed to withstand harsh conditions, including exposure to dust, moisture, and temperature fluctuations, ensuring durability and consistent performance. Their design often includes features that enhance signal integrity and minimize interference, catering to a wide range of industrial applications, such as automation, control systems, and machinery connectivity.	0173-1#01-BAC413#020	27440312	4 			Test plug	a test plug is a specialized device designed for connecting and testing electrical circuits and systems. Typically featuring pins or terminals, a test plug allows for easy connection to testing equipment, facilitating the monitoring of electrical parameters such as voltage, current, and resistance. These plugs are essential in the development, maintenance, and troubleshooting of electronic devices, providing a reliable means to ensure operational integrity. The design and specifications of test plugs can vary depending on their intended application, ensuring compatibility with different types of testing apparatus and circuit configurations.	0173-1#01-ADT244#015	27440313	4 			Connector for luminaire	a device designed to establish a secure and reliable electrical connection between a lighting fixture and a power source or control system. The design ensures that any electrical connections made through this outlet are secure and resistant to environmental factors, providing reliable performance in critical applications. The product is engineered to comply with relevant regulations, making it suitable for use in locations requiring enhanced safety measures.	0173-1#01-ACN447#019	27440511	4 			Ex-proof CEE-plug/-coupler	this category encompasses electrical connectors designed for use in potentially explosive atmospheres. These connectors are engineered to meet stringent safety standards, ensuring that they can operate safely in environments where flammable gases or dust may be present. The devices in this classification are typically constructed with robust materials and include features that prevent the ignition of hazardous substances. They are utilized in various industrial applications, particularly in sectors such as oil and gas, chemical processing, and mining, where safety and reliability are paramount. The classification distinguishes these connectors from standard variants by their enhanced protection mechanisms and compliance with specific regulations governing explosive environments.	0173-1#01-AKE324#021	27440512	4 			HNA plug	this classification encompasses a specific type of electrical connector designed for reliable and secure plug connections in various applications. Typically employed in industrial, commercial, or specialized environments, these connectors are engineered to meet stringent safety standards and may include features such as weatherproofing or enhanced insulation. The design allows for easy installation and maintenance while ensuring compatibility with various equipment requiring substantial electrical power.
